steps.title: Find the time anchor
steps.content: The sentence places the action in a specific historical moment (“In 1613”) and immediately coordinates it with a past-tense verb: “ignited.” This setup requires the blank verb to use past tense.

steps.title: Apply tense consistency
steps.content: When describing a sequence of historical actions in the same time period, all verbs must use simple past. B) malfunctioned uses simple past and aligns with “ignited.” The other choices use present perfect (A), future (C), or simple present (D), none of which maintain consistency with the established past tense. That matches choice B — B is correct.
